how: Moved bounds selection, fallback cap, and batch marker creation into an owner-local helper.
problem: The Worker API returned data, but the front end iterated raw KTO candidates in one effect and created map markers too aggressively.
remainingGap: Graphify source graph freshness remained a governance follow-up.
why: Prevent mobile renderer stalls from unbounded KTO marker creation.

how: Added NaverMarkerConfig materialization limits and center-distance cap selection.
problem: Normal Naver map bounds could still allow too many KTO candidates to materialize.
remainingGap: Graphify freshness was recorded as separate governance evidence.
why: Keep production KTO marker materialization under a mobile-safe cap.

how: Added E2E coverage for dropdown opening, screenshot success, and no tourism API call.
problem: The all filter should open without API calls, but production showed renderer freeze or screenshot timeout.
remainingGap: Initial KTO full load performance stayed in a separate hardening scope.
why: Prevent map floating capsule click freeze and Naver default control overlap.

how: Changed caps, batch size, requestAnimationFrame scheduling, and diff-based SDK mutation.
problem: Large marker insertion could still cause screenshot timeout and renderer freeze.
remainingGap: Payload slimming, edge cache, and server-side viewport query remain backend candidates.
why: Keep KTO map interactions responsive under the 300ms interaction target.

how: Made total required, kept KTO ON to one scope all call, kept filters local, and lazy-loaded detail by id.
problem: The list response total was optional and snapshot not-ready states were not fixed as retryable disabled KTO layer states.
remainingGap: Marker click detail sheet is not covered in E2E without a Naver client id, so lazy-load is covered through hook tests.
why: Align Web Front with the KTO scope all KV snapshot compact read model.

how: Updated repo QA matrix, 1.3.5 release candidate, completion report, Wiki release and QA pages, and recorded PR, merge SHA, CI URL, concrete test seam, and parent child issue evidence.
problem: TSK-015 implementation PRs were merged, but QA matrix, release candidate, Wiki navigation, task ledger, issue tree, and parent child evidence were not connected under one completion standard.
remainingGap: No traceability gap remains for this scope. Official tag and GitHub Release publication remain separate release work.
why: Record sixth UIUX drawer, bottom navigation, and PWA icon redesign evidence across docs, Wiki, QA matrix, release candidate, and task ledger.
Record current responsibility boundaries and missing components before implementing the seventh UIUX global capsule and expandable action architecture.
problem: AppHeader and MapFloatingNav split global utility and capsule responsibilities, while AppCapsule, SideDrawer, and SpeedDialFAB did not exist yet.
how: Added the TSK-016 governance index rows and fixed the current AppShell, AppHeader, MapFloatingNav, GlobalSettingsMenu, BottomNav, and MapBottomSheet baseline through an audit document and source-read test.
remainingGap: Actual AppCapsule, SideDrawer, and SpeedDialFAB implementation remains in issues 510, 512, and 513. The local npm test:e2e wrapper hang remains an E2E runner stability gap, while direct Playwright and remote CI passed.

how: prop 기반 AppCapsule을 추가하고, GlobalSettingsMenu를 right action slot에서 재사용했으며, window.history, /settings, ti-* 결합이 생기지 않도록 테스트와 source guard를 추가했다.
problem: 후속 capsule center-control migration, SideDrawer, SpeedDialFAB 작업이 참조할 안정적인 전역 capsule shell contract가 없었다.
remainingGap: center control migration은 TSK-016-03, SideDrawer shell은 TSK-016-04, SpeedDialFAB는 TSK-016-05에 남아 있다.
why: 지도 전용 capsule control을 전역 네비게이션 아키텍처로 옮기기 전에 공유 AppCapsule boundary를 먼저 고정해야 했다.

how: Composed MapFloatingNav inside AppCapsule.center, removed duplicated menu/settings shell controls from MapFloatingNav, and updated notification anchoring plus E2E selectors.
problem: TSK-016-02 added the AppCapsule shell, but the map screen still rendered the old MapFloatingNav shell.
remainingGap: SideDrawer shell remains in TSK-016-04 and SpeedDialFAB remains in TSK-016-05.
why: Connect map-only capsule controls to the global AppCapsule architecture.

how: Added an empty SideDrawer shell, wired AppCapsule menu open state through App composition, preserved GlobalSettingsMenu behavior, and guarded against placeholder copy, /settings route, window.history.length, ti-* classes, and icon package coupling.
problem: AppCapsule needed a left menu shell target, but the side menu IA and copy were not approved.
remainingGap: SpeedDialFAB action contract remains in TSK-016-05 and capsule/FAB z-index regression remains in TSK-016-06.
why: Allow the global capsule menu trigger to open a governed shell without introducing unapproved menu content or navigation coupling.
